name: Optimize trees fileListToTree linux fixture performance

description: Baseline: linux fixture fileListToTree benchmark via autoresearch.sh (5 repeated benchmark runs, median-of-medians).

asi.hypothesis: Establish a low-noise baseline for linux fixture total median before code changes.

description: Two-pass hash-key remapping with precomputed idByPath lookup (object table) to avoid per-child Map.get in hash stage.

asi.hypothesis: Precomputing IDs then remapping through an object lookup would reduce hashTreeKeys overhead from repeated Map lookups.
asi.result: Strong regression: hash stage ballooned while other stages improved slightly.
asi.rollback_reason: Extra full pass plus object dictionary lookups were more expensive than the original cached-symbol + Map path.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep single-pass hashing; target folder/pathGraph costs next or reduce allocations inside remap loops without adding a second pass.

description: Skip sortChildren work for child arrays of length <=1 inside fileListToTree build context.

asi.hypothesis: Many folders might have <=1 child, so bypassing sort logic there should trim folder/flattening overhead.
asi.result: No win; total median regressed slightly and folder/hash stages drifted up.
asi.rollback_reason: Benefit from skipping tiny-array sorts is too small relative to noise and possible secondary overhead changes.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on pathGraph string/path parsing or hash remap hot loops where absolute time share is larger.

description: Path-graph file node dedupe now uses parent child-set size change as the fast-path uniqueness check, only falling back to tree.has() on collisions.

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding one Map.has call per unique file path should reduce hot path-graph overhead on large fixtures.
asi.result: Large win on primary metric with broad secondary improvements.
asi.insight: On linux workload nearly all file paths are unique; Set membership delta is a cheaper uniqueness signal than consulting tree.has() every time.
asi.expected_safety: Behavior preserved for duplicate files and file/folder-key conflicts via fallback tree.has() branch.
asi.follow_up: Confirm stability with another no-code rerun before stacking additional changes.
